summary: Cycle 1 verdict and core argument remain sound. The dome has no testable mechanism for differential decay rates between the two SAA cells. However, two Cycle 1 corrections were not applied: (1) CHAOS-7 DOI remains incorrect (should be 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9, not 10.1186/s40623-020-01163-9), and (2) monitoring tag contradicts Cycle 1 code analysis (shows 'live_fetch' but Cycle 1 established it should be 'hardcoded'). These are fixable but critical for credibility.

fix_1_chaos7_doi.cycle_1_recommendation: Verify DOI 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 or cite CHAOS-7 directly

fix_2_terra_nova_citation.cycle_1_recommendation: Verify Terra-Nova DOI or focus on CHAOS-7 as primary source
fix_2_terra_nova_citation.status: PARTIALLY APPLIED (CHAOS-7 appears but Terra-Nova still secondary)

fix_3_differential_decay_rates.cycle_1_recommendation: Add specific CHAOS-7 decay rate values for both cells
fix_3_differential_decay_rates.current_state: detail_evidence gives approximate ranges (160-200 nT/yr African, 80-120 nT/yr East Indian)

content_consistency_check.detail_claim_vs_detail_evidence: CONSISTENT. Claim states African cell decays faster; evidence supports this with decay rate differential.
content_consistency_check.verdict_vs_evidence: CONSISTENT. Verdict 'Std Model Explains' correctly reflects that MHD dynamics explain the observation, not the dome's aetheric geometry.
content_consistency_check.detail_verdict_text_clarity: STRONG. Clearly articulates that differential decay requires fluid core mechanism, which dome lacks.
content_consistency_check.dome_mechanism_critique: ACCURATE. Our review correctly identifies that dome conflates geographic SEPARATION (WIN-004) with intensity DECAY RATE (WIN-005). The dome's mechanism explanation for WIN-004 (exponential cell separation) does NOT explain why African cell decays faster in intensity. This conflation is a dome weakness, not our strawman.

monitoring.cycle_1_analysis: Hardcoded. Dome's inject_ai_layer.py contains static metadata with observed values (23,050→21,880 nT). Monitor.py watches single TTB station threshold, not differential cell dynamics.
monitoring.verdict: CURRENT TAG IS INCORRECT. Should be 'hardcoded' per Cycle 1 code audit.
monitoring.dome_reality: The dome does not dynamically fetch CHAOS-7 cell decay rates. It hardcodes a threshold (21,880 nT) and checks if TTB station breaches it. This is threshold-monitoring, not model-driven prediction.

relabels_standard.validation: CORRECT. Dome relabels MHD reversal-flux patches (standard geomagnetism) as 'aetheric field geometry'. The numerical predictions remain unchanged; only terminology shifts.
relabels_standard.evidence: Dome source (02-wins.txt line 36): 'Aetheric field geometry predicts the African cell decays faster because of its position relative to the pole axis.' No new physics, just renamed causation.

post_hoc.validation: CORRECT. Dome observation: CHAOS-7 shows African cell decayed from 23,050 to 21,880 nT by 2026-03-12 (data exists before claim). Dome then invokes geometry to explain.
post_hoc.prospective_claim_status: WIN-005 is listed as 'CONFIRMED' in dome source, not as prospective prediction. PROS-005 (SAA African cell below 21,795 nT) was registered 2026-03-06 and confirmed 2026-03-12 — that is prospective. WIN-005 is retroactive.

derives_from_dome.validation: CORRECT. Dome offers no differential equations or geometric derivation for why one cell decays faster. The exponential curve (WIN-004) describes separation, not decay rate. No dome parameter set produces the observed differential decay from first principles.

reviewed.validation: CORRECT. Both Cycle 1 and Cycle 2 have validated these tags.

dome_defender_angle_1.claim: The dome's aetheric field IS the standard model, just using older nomenclature. If it makes the same predictions, it's equivalent.
dome_defender_angle_1.rebuttal: This fails because equivalence requires either (a) same equations, or (b) different equations with identical predictions across all regimes. The dome has NO equations for differential decay. Standard MHD predicts the observation from core-mantle boundary dynamics, spherical harmonic decomposition, and time-dependent reversal-flux patches. The dome predicts it from 'geometry' without specifying what that geometry is. These are not equivalent — one is testable, one is post-hoc narrative.
dome_defender_angle_1.our_strength: We don't argue the dome is false; we argue it's unfalsifiable and post-hoc.

dome_defender_angle_2.claim: Why should the dome separately predict African cell decay rates? The CHAOS-7 model itself is just curve-fitting Earth data — it's not predicting from first principles either.
dome_defender_angle_2.rebuttal: CHAOS-7 is a spherical harmonic model fitted to satellite and ground magnetometer data. It has testable structure: if you input core-mantle boundary conditions, you can rerun the forward model. The dome's aetheric geometry has no rerunnable model. Also, CHAOS-7 is built on physics (Maxwell's equations + MHD): if you change core dynamics (e.g., change reversal-flux patch morphology), the predictions change. The dome's geometry is fixed post-hoc to match data.
dome_defender_angle_2.our_strength: CHAOS-7 is transparent and testable; dome geometry is not.

dome_defender_angle_3.claim: The dome registered a prospective prediction (PROS-005: African cell below 21,795 nT), which was confirmed. Isn't that predictive success?
dome_defender_angle_3.rebuttal: PROS-005 was registered 2026-03-06 and confirmed 2026-03-12 — 6 days later. The threshold 21,795 nT was 'derived from the WIN-005 exponential decay curve extrapolated forward' (dome source line 36). This is not a prospective prediction; it is a retroactive threshold extracted from an already-observed decay curve and tested on data 6 days later. True prospective prediction requires: (a) registered before data collection, (b) threshold NOT derived from the same data. PROS-005 fails both tests.
dome_defender_angle_3.our_strength: The decider should flag PROS-005 as post-hoc retroactive verification, not prospective prediction.

kernel_of_truth: The African SAA cell does decay faster than the Atlantic/East-Indian cell (CHAOS-7 confirms ~160-200 nT/yr vs ~80-120 nT/yr). The dome recognizes this asymmetry exists and correctly observes it in the data. The kernel of truth is the observation itself, not the dome's mechanism. The dome claims aetheric geometry explains this; reality is it arises from MHD reversal-flux patches in the outer core, a solved problem with rigorous physics. The dome's mechanism is non-falsifiable and adds no explanatory power.

holes_found.issue: CHAOS-7 DOI is incorrect in detail_evidence
holes_found.evidence: Current wins.json: https://doi.org/10.1186/s40623-020-01163-9. Cycle 1 identified correct DOI: 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9. These are different papers in the same journal.
holes_found.impact: Citation becomes unverifiable. Readers cannot check the source. Credibility suffers, even though the claim (CHAOS-7 shows differential decay) is factually correct.
holes_found.recommendation: Change DOI to 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 immediately. This is a Cycle 1 fix that was not applied.

holes_found.evidence: Cycle 1 established (dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed): 'Hardcoded. The African cell value is listed as a static entry in inject_ai_layer.py with no visible calculation.' Current wins.json shows monitoring='live_fetch'.
holes_found.impact: Misleads readers about dome's actual infrastructure. If monitoring='live_fetch', readers assume dome dynamically tracks CHAOS-7 updates. Reality: dome hardcodes observed thresholds and monitors TTB station. This is a significant semantic difference.
holes_found.recommendation: Change to monitoring='hardcoded'. The dome does not fetch live CHAOS-7 model outputs; it hardcodes observed values.

holes_found.issue: Terra-Nova et al. DOI may still be incorrect
holes_found.evidence: Current wins.json: 10.1016/j.epsl.2017.06.036 (Earth and Planetary Science Letters). Cycle 1 noted alternative: 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002 (Physics of the Earth and Planetary Interiors). Cycle 1 recommended 'cite CHAOS-7 and Olsen et al. (2020) directly as the primary source.'
holes_found.impact: Secondary to CHAOS-7 citation. If Terra-Nova DOI is wrong, readers may not find the paper, but our core argument (reversed-flux patches explain asymmetry) is sound regardless.
holes_found.recommendation: Either verify the correct Terra-Nova DOI, or remove it and cite CHAOS-7 alone as the primary evidence source.

holes_found.issue: Could strengthen explanation of why reversed-flux patches cause differential decay
holes_found.evidence: Current text explains that reversed-flux patches generate splitting and differential rates, but does not explain the physical mechanism (e.g., how a reversed patch at one location decays differently than a normal patch at another).
holes_found.impact: Low. The core argument (dome has no mechanism vs. standard model has testable mechanism) is not undermined. But a more detailed explanation would strengthen credibility.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: add brief mechanism note — e.g., 'Reversed-flux patches (RFPs) at the core-mantle boundary decay at different rates due to differences in outer-core advection speed and thermal diffusivity around each patch. The African cell's RFP decays faster because of its location within the stronger equatorward flow in the South Atlantic sector.'

stronger_arguments.argument: Defender: 'The dome's aetheric monopole at the apex naturally creates two minima in the equatorial plane, and their decay rates differ due to distance-from-apex differences.'
stronger_arguments.rebuttal: A monopole field in any geometry produces a SINGLE extremum (minimum or maximum), not two separate minima. The dome's mechanism for creating TWO cells is never explained. Standard geomagnetism produces this through non-dipolar terms in spherical harmonic decomposition — but the dome model is fundamentally a monopole (or at best a dipole at the apex), not a multipole expansion. Two minima require either: (1) higher-order multipoles (which dome never specifies), or (2) topological features (which dome calls 'aetheric geometry' without defining). Either way, it's untestable.
stronger_arguments.our_strength: We could strengthen this by explicitly noting that a monopole cannot produce two minima, period. The dome's claim to explain both cells contradicts basic field theory.

stronger_arguments.argument: Defender: 'The dome's exponential decay curve (WIN-004) automatically implies different decay rates if the two cells separate exponentially — they can't both decay at the same absolute rate and maintain exponential separation.'
stronger_arguments.rebuttal: This confuses angular separation with intensity. If two minima separate exponentially in longitude while both decay in intensity, the rate of intensity decay is INDEPENDENT of the rate of angular separation. Both could decay at the same nT/year rate while maintaining exponential angular separation. The dome claims the African cell decays FASTER (160-200 nT/yr) than the Atlantic cell (80-120 nT/yr). This intensity asymmetry is not explained by geometric separation. The dome offers no equation linking the two.
stronger_arguments.our_strength: Our review correctly identifies that the dome conflates two different physical processes (geographic separation and intensity decay) without connecting them.

stronger_arguments.argument: Defender: 'CHAOS-7 is also a model fitting data, not a prediction from first principles. Why is CHAOS-7's mechanism more valid than the dome's?'
stronger_arguments.rebuttal: CHAOS-7's mechanism is rooted in Maxwell's equations and MHD theory. The core-mantle boundary dynamics are well-understood: buoyancy-driven convection in the outer core, Coriolis forces, and magnetic field line topology. If you change the initial conditions (e.g., different iron composition, different heat flux), CHAOS-7's equations will produce different predictions. The dome's mechanism cannot be rerun with different assumptions because 'aetheric geometry' is not defined. Testability is the difference.
stronger_arguments.our_strength: CHAOS-7 is transparent and falsifiable; the dome is not.

verdict_assessment.verdict_rationale: 'Std Model Explains' is the correct category. The standard model (MHD core dynamics) explains the differential decay rates from first principles. The dome's claim is non-falsifiable (aetheric geometry is never specified) and post-hoc (fits after observation). We are not saying the dome is false; we are saying it offers no testable mechanism and no prospective predictive power.

alternative_verdicts_considered.why_not: The differential decay rates ARE observed; the observation is not refuted. We critique the dome's mechanism, not the observation.
This would apply if the African cell did NOT decay faster. It does. The observation is demonstrated. We critique the dome's explanation.

This could apply if the dome cherry-picked data or mislabeled observations. The decay rates are correctly stated. The misleading part is the claim of mechanism, which 'Std Model Explains' better captures.

cycle_2_findings_summary: Verdict is robust and well-supported. Core argument (dome lacks testable mechanism) is sound. However, two critical issues from Cycle 1 remain unfixed: (1) wrong CHAOS-7 DOI, (2) monitoring tag contradicts code analysis. The monitoring tag is particularly important because it misrepresents how the dome actually works. These fixes are quick but essential for credibility. The dome's conflation of geographic separation (WIN-004) with intensity decay rate (WIN-005) is a genuine weakness that our review correctly identifies.

specific_edits_needed.change: Verify Terra-Nova DOI or remove in favor of CHAOS-7 as primary source

sources_checked: https://doi.org/10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 (CHAOS-7 model, Olsen et al. 2020) — CORRECT DOI
https://doi.org/10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002 (Terra-Nova et al., alternative noted in Cycle 1 — ambiguous)
